title: coronilla minima var. hispanica
intro: Coronilla minima var. hispanica, also known as Spanish clover or little coronilla, is a perennial plant belonging to the pea family and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Coronilla minima var. hispanica has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
